title: Adult CPR (Hands-Only)
immediate_steps: Call emergency services
Place the person flat on their back on a hard surface
Kneel beside them
Place the heel of one hand on the center of the chest (lower half of the sternum) and the other hand on top
Interlock your fingers
Keep arms straight and push hard and fast (at least 2 inches deep, 100-120 compressions per minute)
Allow the chest to return to normal position after each compression
Continue until help arrives or an AED is ready
avoid: Do not stop compressions for more than 10 seconds
Do not lean on the chest between compressions

title: Child CPR (1 to Puberty)
immediate_steps: Call emergency services
Place the child on a firm, flat surface
Use one or two hands in the center of the chest
Push down about 2 inches (5 cm) at a rate of 100-120 per minute
If trained, give 2 rescue breaths after every 30 compressions
Tilt the head back slightly and lift the chin for breaths
avoid: Do not tilt the head back too far
Do not press on the bottom tip of the breastbone
Do not give large breaths (just enough to make the chest rise)

title: Infant CPR (Under 1 year)
immediate_steps: Flick the bottom of the foot to check for responsiveness
Call emergency services
Place the infant on a firm, flat surface
Place two fingers in the center of the chest, just below the nipple line
Push down about 1.5 inches (4 cm) at a rate of 100-120 per minute
If trained, give 2 gentle rescue breaths for every 30 compressions (cover both mouth and nose with your mouth)
avoid: Do not push too deep (max 1.5 inches)
Do not breathe forcefully (puff only from your cheeks)

title: CPR - Immediate Assessment and Response
immediate_steps: Ensure the area is safe before approaching the victim
Check for responsiveness by tapping and shouting
If there is no response, call emergency services right away
Check breathing for no more than 10 seconds
If the person is not breathing or only gasping, begin CPR immediately

title: CPR - Chest Compressions Technique
immediate_steps: Place the heel of one hand on the center of the chest between the nipples and place the other hand on top
Keep arms straight and press hard and fast at a rate of about 100 to 120 compressions per minute
Push down at least 5 centimeters in adults and allow full chest recoil after each compression
Minimize interruptions to maintain blood flow to vital organs
Continue compressions until emergency help arrives or the person shows signs of life

title: CPR - Rescue Breathing Basics
immediate_steps: After 30 chest compressions, open the airway by tilting the head back and lifting the chin
Pinch the nose and give two rescue breaths by sealing your mouth over the victim's mouth and blowing steadily until the chest rises
Each breath should last about one second
If the chest does not rise, reposition the head and try again
Continue cycles of 30 compressions and 2 breaths if trained to do so
If untrained, continue hands-only CPR with chest compressions only
